core_mechanism: a percolation/connectivity transition on a directed network controlled by a spectral threshold, where the giant connected component collapses exactly when the largest eigenvalue of the retention-reweighted adjacency matrix (entries A_ij(1-p_i)) drops below one
brief_reason: The abstract predicts the network disintegrates when the leading eigenvalue of the matrix with entries A_ij(1-p_i) falls below 1, making the percolation threshold a spectral (largest-eigenvalue-crosses-unity) criterion on the adjacency operator.
